title: Novavax Q3 2023 Earnings: Beats on Results but Cuts Sales View Due to Market Softness
text: Novavax reported third-quarter 2023 loss of $1.26 cents per share, which was narrower than the Zacks Consensus Estimate of a loss of $1.82. Revenues in the quarter amounted to $187.0 million, topping the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $165.1 million. Novavax received Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in October for its updated COVID-19 vaccine and is executing its commercial strategy to maximize access for consumers who want a protein-based non-mRNA vaccine option. Novavax's vaccine is available at approximately 14,000 pharmacy locations, including Costco, CVS Pharmacy, Giant, Publix, Rite Aid and Stop & Shop. Novavax expects to record total revenues of around $300 million in first-quarter 2024, entirely from product sales. Additionally, Novavax is prepared to initiate additional cost reductions to decrease 2024 expenses by over $300 million above and beyond the previously stated targets for 2024. The cost-cutting initiatives signal management's concern about future revenue sustainability and the need to align operations with expected market demand.
